classes.name: Operator input and conversation content
classes.handling: Treat prompts, channel messages, transcripts, and assistant responses as user data. Persist only through session/message stores or explicit artifact paths; never leak across channel scopes without consent.

classes.name: Runtime configuration
classes.handling: Configuration controls model order, channels, workspace paths, agency/autonomy, and runtime behavior. Mutations must use central config/policy seams with validation and audit for authority-bearing settings.

classes.name: Provider and integration secrets
classes.handling: API keys, channel tokens, billing account-adjacent secrets, and mail credentials must remain in keystore or explicit secret storage. UI and logs must not echo secret values.

classes.name: Agent memory records
classes.handling: Working, episodic, semantic, and indexed memory can contain private user context. Ingest, retrieval, curation, and repair must be scoped to the configured store and isolated during tests.

classes.name: Tool outputs and file artifacts
classes.handling: Tool results, artifact proofs, file writes, and workspace observations require typed evidence. Claims about artifacts must be backed by matching tool or filesystem proof.

classes.name: Telemetry, traces, logs, and scorecards
classes.handling: Operational telemetry should include model route, latency breakdown, stage health, retries, and failures while avoiding secret leakage. It supports diagnostics, user transparency, and release gates.

classes.name: Scheduler and cron state
classes.handling: Cron definitions, leases, due executions, failures, and notifications are durable operational data. Scheduler business logic must remain centralized and must not bypass pipeline behavior.

classes.name: Voice, audio, image, and camera media
classes.handling: Captured media requires explicit permission, provenance, retention policy, and canonical multimodal message parts. STT, TTS, camera policy, and media interpretation belong in runtime services.

classes.name: Mail and calendar-adjacent content
classes.handling: Email bodies, headers, mailbox metadata, outbound mail targets, and bridge credentials require explicit operator configuration and careful logging redaction.

classes.name: Billing account and financial state
classes.handling: Billing account addresses, balances, metered-billing/payment metadata, and transaction-adjacent data require conservative display, confirmation, and audit boundaries.

classes.name: Public documentation and catalog metadata
classes.handling: Public docs, skill/plugin catalog metadata, themes, and non-secret release metadata may be cached and rendered broadly, but source integrity still matters.
